meta.reportLabel: Fri - BOARD CONSOLIDATES: the soft-USD leg (the last one working) PAUSED - Thu's resilient data (jobless claims 208k, a 2-mo low; retail sales +0.2%) bounced DXY back to ~100.7 and firmed Sept-hike odds to ~44%. It's a pause, not a reversal (USD still weekly-lower). Gold broke <$4,000 (~$3,984) and silver slid to ~$56 as the haven unwind extended; oil stayed capped (WTI ~$79.6) DESPITE worsening US-Iran strikes. Short USD/CAD loses its 2nd leg and downgrades HIGH->MED (one leg now). Gold/silver -> RANGE->short tilt. All oil-linked hostage to a STRAINED Sat Jul 18 Oman meeting. Reduce size into the weekend.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: A relief bounce trims the board's last clean edge. The one leg still working - the soft-USD slide - paused: Thursday's resilient US data (jobless claims 208k, a 2-mo low; retail sales +0.2% m/m) bounced DXY back to ~100.6-100.7 after two down days and firmed Sept-hike odds to ~44%. It's a pause within a still-weekly-lower dollar, not a reversal, but it removes the momentum carrying the board. Gold broke below $4,000 (~$3,984) and silver slid to ~$56 as the haven unwind extended, while oil stayed capped (WTI ~$79.6, Brent ~$84.6) DESPITE worsening US-Iran strikes. With the USD leg pausing and oil capped, short USD/CAD loses its cleanest edge and downgrades HIGH->MED (one leg now); the board is broadly RANGE with no clean two-leg trade, all oil-linked hostage to a strained Sat Jul 18 Oman meeting.
meta.nextBigEvent: Sat Jul 18 Iran-Oman Hormuz talks are the imminent - but now STRAINED - off-ramp: Oman drafted a tentative traffic proposal, yet the strait is the stumbling block and Iran accuses the US of blocking a result. A deal crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium in minutes; a breakdown re-spikes it. Then Mon Jul 21 NZ Q2 CPI (ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5%, above RBNZ's 3.9% call) is the NZD swing, with Jul 23 ECB and Jul 28-29 FOMC framing the back half of the month.

dailyRead: The one leg that was still working - the soft-USD slide - took a breather, and that turns the whole board into consolidation. Thursday's US data leaned resilient: initial jobless claims fell to a two-month low of 208k (below the ~217k call) and, though June retail sales slowed to +0.2% m/m (from +1.0%), the labour signal was firm enough to pause the dollar's decline - DXY bounced back to ~100.6-100.7 after two straight down days, and markets trimmed September-hike odds to ~44%. It is a pause within a still-weekly-lower dollar, not a reversal - but it removes the momentum that had been carrying the board. Meanwhile the two cooling legs kept bleeding: gold broke below $4,000 (spot ~$3,984) and silver slid to ~$56 as the haven unwind extended, while oil stayed capped - WTI ~$79.6, Brent ~$84.6 - despite worsening US-Iran strikes (US hit an oil tanker near Iran's main export terminal; Hormuz traffic dwindling). Net: with the USD leg pausing and oil capped, short USD/CAD loses its cleanest edge and downgrades from HIGH to MED - CAD stays strongest on the BoC's oil-conditional hawkish hold, but yesterday's one-and-a-half-leg trade is now a one-leg consolidation. The board is broadly RANGE with no clean two-leg trade. Cleanest leans: short USD/CAD stays best of a consolidating board (MED, one leg), CAD/JPY LONG holds on the oil-divergence + a weaker yen (~162.4), and gold/silver shift to RANGE->short tilt. Reduce size into the weekend binary.

today.headline: Relief bounce - resilient claims (208k, a 2-mo low) paused the slide; DXY back to ~100.7, Sept-hike odds ~44%
today.read: The soft-USD leg, the one thing still working, took a breather - a pause within a still-weekly-lower dollar, not a reversal. It removes the momentum carrying the board and trims short USD/CAD to one leg. Risk: a soft payroll re-arms the slide; hot July oil-inflation data extends the bounce into the Jul 28-29 FOMC.

today.headline: Still strongest on the oil-hawk BoC, but short USD/CAD downgrades HIGH->MED as the USD-short leg paused and oil stays capped
today.read: CAD keeps the crown (Macklem: hikes possible IF oil spikes; growth resumed), but both USD/CAD drivers stalled - the USD bounced and oil is capped (~$79.6). USD/CAD SHORT is now a one-leg grind toward ~1.40 (MED); CAD/JPY LONG (MED) holds on the oil-divergence. Risk: a Sat Oman deal crushes oil AND flips the BoC dovish.

today.headline: Drifted weaker toward ~162.4 (a 4-decade low) on the USD bounce + oil bill - back toward the MoF zone
today.read: The firmer dollar and the oil bill nudged USD/JPY toward ~162.4, above the April intervention level; MoF stays verbal-only, the Y11.73tn precedent unused. It firms the XXX/JPY carry-longs' yen leg. RANGE on USD/JPY; CAD/JPY LONG holds. Risk: another leg to the MoF zone triggers a strike; a panic snaps the carry.

today.headline: No change - the USD bounce cancels the softer-oil relief tilt; EUR/USD ~1.14 into Jul 23 ECB
today.read: The softer-oil terms-of-trade relief is offset today by the firmer dollar - back to a two-way with no clean vector. Jul 23 ECB is the next test. Risk: a hawkish ECB core tone hands it a leg; a re-firing dollar or an oil re-spike re-pins it lower.

today.headline: No change - hot Q2 CPI risk holds (ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5%, Jul 21), but the USD bounce trims the relief
today.read: The swing catalyst is firm and above the RBNZ's 3.9% call; a hot Jul 21 print re-arms the hawks. But the USD relief bounce caps the NZD relief bid. NZD/JPY tilts up; NZD/CAD short holds on oil. Risk: an oil risk-off leg hits high-beta NZD; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike.

today.headline: No change - firm rate story (services 3.7%), but the USD bounce trims the cable tailwind; UK June CPI ~Jul 22
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, but the dollar's relief bounce trims the tailwind cable enjoyed the prior two days. GBP/USD RANGE; GBP/JPY carry-long tilt holds. Risk: a risk-off flush; a firmer USD; a soft UK CPI (~Jul 22).

today.headline: No change - now capped on both sides (firm USD + oil-risk); Jun jobs ~Jul 23
today.read: Sticky-core floor, but the firmer USD and the oil-shock risk-off tail cap it both ways. Thin Aug hike tail (~36%), China CPI +1%. MIXED. Risk: a re-escalation leg re-softens it; a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) + an Oman deal re-arm the hawks.

strength.verdict: STRONGEST — oil-hawk BoC; USD-short leg paused
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (gasoline-driven; ex-gas 2.2%, core ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5%); BoC held 2.25% (6th straight) - Macklem: hikes still possible IF oil spikes, growth 'looks to have resumed' (0.7% GDP 2026). CAD stays the strongest currency, but both USD/CAD drivers stalled today: the USD bounced (DXY ~100.7) and oil is capped (~$79.6).
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The oil-conditional hawkish BoC keeps CAD strongest, but with the USD-short leg paused and oil capped, USD/CAD short is a one-leg grind toward ~1.40, not a rocket. Risk: a Sat Oman deal crushes oil AND flips BoC dovish; a firmer USD; a risk-off flush hits CAD's risk leg. Next BoC Sep (data-dependent).

strength.verdict: FIRM — rate story; USD-bounce trims the tailwind
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). The dollar's relief bounce trims the cable tailwind it enjoyed the prior two days; risk-off keeps GBP a high-beta passenger.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30, but the USD bounce trims the near-term tailwind. Risk: a risk-off flush hits it; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the domestic check; a firmer USD. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.drivers: June CPI -0.4% m/m -> 3.5% y/y (from 4.2%); core FLAT m/m -> 2.6%. NFP +57k (Jun); but jobless claims fell to a 2-mo low of 208k and retail sales +0.2% m/m - resilient. Fed 3.50-3.75% hold. July-hike odds ~12%, Sept ~44%; DXY bounced to ~100.7 after two down days, still on track for a weekly decline.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The disinflation repricing is still the trend (DXY weekly-lower), but resilient claims paused the slide (DXY ~100.7, Sept odds ~44%) - a dovish lean that stopped falling into the Jul 28-29 FOMC (still a hold). Risk: hot July oil-inflation data extends the bounce; a soft payroll re-arms the slide. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.verdict: FIRM — hot Q2 CPI risk confirmed
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, but scripted dovish: headline seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2. But ANZ now sees Q2 CPI 4.4%, BNZ 4.5% (Jul 21) - ABOVE that call - and 1-yr inflation expectations at 3.41% (2-yr 2.53%). A hawkish risk. The USD relief bounce trims the near-term relief bid.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The Jul 21 Q2 CPI is the swing - a hot print (4.4-4.5%) re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike. Risk: an oil-shock risk-off leg hits high-beta NZD; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike; an Oman deal. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. EUR/USD ~1.14; the softer-oil terms-of-trade relief is offset today by the firmer dollar - back to a two-way with no clean vector.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Softer-oil relief cancelled by the USD bounce = a two-way into Jul 23 ECB. Risk: a hawkish ECB core tone hands EUR a leg; a re-firing dollar or an oil re-spike re-pins it lower. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp ~4.3% loosening (June jobs ~Jul 23). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. Sticky-core floor, but the firmer USD and the oil-shock risk-off tail now cap it on both sides.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Firm USD + oil-risk cap = two-way. Risk: a re-escalation leg re-softens it; an Oman deal + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arm the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.verdict: WEAK — drifts toward the MoF zone (~162.4)
strength.tag: USD bounce + oil bill; MoF zone near
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. The USD bounce + the oil bill nudged USD/JPY back toward ~162.4 (a 4-decade low, above the April intervention level). MoF verbal-only, Y11.73tn precedent unused.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 The USD bounce + oil bill drift it toward the MoF zone; BoJ Jul 31 hawkish is the offset. Risk: another leg to the MoF zone triggers a strike; a genuine panic snaps the carry trade. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.why: Best of a consolidating board, now one leg: CAD strongest (BoC oil-hawk + growth resumed) and USD still weekly-lower toward ~1.40 - but the USD-short leg paused (DXY ~100.7) and oil is capped (~$79.6).
symbols.risk: A Sat Oman deal crushes oil and flips BoC dovish; a firmer USD extends the bounce; a risk-off flush hits CAD.

symbols.why: The oil-divergence trade, helped by a weaker yen: oil-supported CAD (BoC oil-hawk) vs the ~100% energy-importer JPY drifting toward ~162.4 - the oil leg still splits them.
symbols.risk: A Sat Oman deal crushes oil and CAD; a JPY haven/panic snap or a MoF strike lifts the yen faster.

symbols.why: WTI ~$79.6 / Brent ~$84.6 - the blockade, dwindling Hormuz traffic + a fresh US tanker strike keep a floor, but oil would NOT rise on worsening strikes as spare capacity, demand fear & Oman mediation cap it.
symbols.risk: A Sat Oman deal unwinds it fast; a fresh Hormuz closure or wider tanker war re-spikes it.

symbols.why: The haven unwind extended - gold broke below $4,000 (~$3,984); the geopolitics leg cooled and the USD bounced; only soft real rates keep a floor. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hawkish FOMC repricing lifts real rates; an Oman deal deflates it; a fresh escalation re-fires it.

symbols.why: The USD relief bounce cancels the softer-oil terms-of-trade tilt - back to two-way ~1.14.
symbols.risk: A hawkish ECB Jul 23 helps; a firmer USD or oil re-spike re-pins it lower.

symbols.why: Firm-rate GBP but the USD bounce trims the cable tailwind - high-beta passenger.
symbols.risk: A risk-off flush hits it; a firmer USD; a soft UK CPI (~Jul 22) undercuts the rate story.

symbols.why: The USD bounce + oil bill nudged it back toward ~162.4 near the MoF zone; lower US real rates still cap the top.
symbols.risk: Oil re-accelerates and grinds it toward 163/the MoF zone; a soft-USD resumption caps it; a MoF strike.

symbols.why: Weak-JPY carry firms it, but the firm USD + oil-risk cap AUD - offsetting, no clean vector.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg hits AUD; a MoF/haven snap lifts JPY faster.

symbols.why: Hot-Q2-CPI-risk NZD + weak carry JPY - mild long tilt, capped by the firmer USD.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg hits NZD; a sub-4% NZ CPI; a MoF/haven snap.

symbols.why: Laggard EUR vs an NZD carrying a confirmed hot-CPI hawkish risk - mild short tilt.
symbols.risk: A sub-4% NZ CPI lifts EUR; a hawkish ECB Jul 23.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a weak carry JPY drifting toward 162.4 - carry-long tilt holds.
symbols.risk: A MoF/haven snap down; a risk-off flush hits GBP.

symbols.why: Both energy-importers, but the yen is the weaker leg toward 162.4 - mild carry-long tilt.
symbols.risk: A hawkish ECB Jul 23; a JPY haven snap; an oil re-spike hits both.

symbols.why: Oil-supported CAD (BoC oil-hawk) vs a doubly-capped AUD (firm USD + oil-risk) - the oil leg still splits them, though consolidation narrows it.
symbols.risk: An Oman deal crushes oil and re-pins CAD; a hot AU CPI lifts AUD.

symbols.why: Oil-supported CAD vs NZD - the oil leg leads, though a confirmed-hot NZ Q2 CPI narrows it.
symbols.risk: An Oman deal; a hot NZ Q2 CPI Jul 21 lifts NZD.

symbols.why: Sticky-core AUD vs a bounced USD - the relief pop + oil-risk cap the mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg; a firmer USD bounce; Jun jobs ~Jul 23.

symbols.why: Confirmed-hot-Q2-CPI NZD vs a bounced USD - the relief pop caps it; risk-off caps.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg; a sub-4% NZ CPI Jul 21; a firmer USD bounce.

symbols.why: Both high-beta, but NZD carries the hotter, now-confirmed near-term CPI catalyst - mild short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hot AU Q2 CPI flips it; a sub-4% NZ CPI.

symbols.why: Laggard EUR vs a doubly-capped AUD - two-way, no clean vector.
symbols.risk: AU Q2 CPI late July; a hawkish ECB Jul 23.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a capped AUD - mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: A hot AU CPI; a risk-on melt-up lifts AUD; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs an NZD with a confirmed hot-CPI hawkish risk - two firm legs, edge thin.
symbols.risk: A hot NZ Q2 CPI Jul 21 lifts NZD; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: Bounced to ~100.7 after two down days as claims (208k, 2-mo low) firmed the data and Sept odds rose to ~44%; still on track for a weekly decline - a pause, not a turn.
symbols.risk: A soft payroll re-arms the slide; hot July oil-inflation data extends the bounce.

symbols.why: The yen drifted weaker toward ~162.4 on the USD bounce + oil bill, so the basket leans lower - the opposite ticket to the XXX/JPY carry-longs.
symbols.risk: A genuine escalation panic hands JPY a real haven bid and firms the basket; a MoF strike.

symbols.why: The oil pullback + cooling geopolitics prop the record-stretched (~25,800) melt-up, but the firmer USD + a still-hot war cap it - roughly offset.
symbols.risk: An oil re-acceleration re-arms the energy-cost headwind; an Oman deal + soft-USD re-fire the melt-up.

symbols.why: Silver slid to ~$56 as the haven bid faded with gold and the firmer USD bit; the industrial-demand leg is a two-sided growth-fear wildcard. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg hits industrial demand; a gold-led haven re-fire lifts it.

catalysts.note: Claims fell to a 2-mo low of 208k (below ~217k); retail sales slowed to +0.2% m/m. Resilient enough to pause the USD slide and firm Sept-hike odds to ~44%.

catalysts.event: Trump's 20% Hormuz toll + US naval blockade
catalysts.note: Blockade of Iranian ports in force; a demanded 20% toll on ALL Hormuz cargo - a structural supply-cost shock if enforced.

catalysts.event: Strait of Hormuz - US/Iran strikes WORSENING
catalysts.note: US hit an oil tanker near Iran's main export terminal; Hormuz shipping traffic dwindling; Iran hit US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain/Jordan/Oman/Qatar - every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.

Oman drafted a tentative Hormuz-traffic proposal, but the strait is the stumbling block and the process is faltering; Iran accuses the US of blocking a result - a deal crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium, a breakdown re-spikes it.
ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5% - ABOVE RBNZ's 'peaked 3.9%' call; a hot print re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike.
Hold; core-2.5% + the oil-inflation spike is the Sept-hike signal.
Unemployment ~4.3% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Warsh round 2; hold; July-hike odds ~12% but resilient claims firm Sept odds to ~44%.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162.4 and the MoF zone near; the oil bill still the drag.

geopolitics: The war is worsening but oil still won't follow it up - that remains the key tell, and it is why the whole board is consolidating. US and Iranian forces intensified strikes overnight: US forces hit an oil tanker near Iran's main export terminal and continued to degrade Iranian command centres, air-defence and missile/drone sites, while Hormuz shipping traffic is visibly dwindling and Iran's strikes targeted US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan, Oman and Qatar. The mid-June interim truce is scrapped. The one off-ramp is live but straining: an Iranian delegation is in Oman, and Muscat has drafted a tentative proposal to manage traffic through the strait - but the strait itself is the stumbling block, the process is faltering, and Iran accuses Washington of 'overt and covert pressure' to block a result. Crucially, oil would not rally even on the worse news: WTI held ~$79.6 (Aug) and Brent ~$84.6 (Sept), roughly flat. The market is treating the Hormuz premium as capped - stacked OPEC+/Saudi/Iran spare capacity, demand-destruction fear and the shaky Oman mediation offset each escalation headline. FX transmission (Friday - the board consolidates): the one driver still moving - the soft dollar - paused. Thursday's resilient data (claims 208k, a 2-mo low; retail sales +0.2%) lifted DXY back to ~100.6-100.7 and firmed Sept-hike odds to ~44%, though the dollar is still set for a weekly decline - a pause, not a turn. That removes the second leg from short USD/CAD: CAD stays the FX winner on the BoC's oil-conditional hawkish hold, but with the USD leg stalled and oil capped, the trade drops HIGH->MED and becomes a one-leg grind toward ~1.40. Gold broke below $4,000 (~$3,984) and silver slid to ~$56 as the haven unwind extended - so gold and silver shift to a RANGE->short tilt. JPY drifted weaker toward ~162.4, nudging it back toward the MoF zone and firming the XXX/JPY carry-longs' yen leg. EUR loses its softer-oil relief tilt to the firmer dollar (~1.14, two-way). AUD/NZD are now capped on both sides (firm USD + oil-risk). GER40 steadies as the oil pullback offsets a firmer USD. Everything oil/haven-linked stays hostage to the Saturday Oman headline - a deal crushes the premium in minutes; a breakdown or a fresh tanker strike re-spikes it. Into a weekend binary with a stalling off-ramp, reduce size.
